name: scenario_b03905_criminal_procedure_vendor_patch_marrowbridge_lib_eq
premise: the incident command structure is under pressure in Q4 2025: 8 critical issues have surfaced over 48 hours, and the response will set a policy precedent for Criminal Procedure. The immediate incident is a vendor patch with unknown side effects. The trigger is that a vendor patch was applied under emergency conditions, and downstream anomalies now look like either tampering or an unintended regression. The board has pre-approved up to $8.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented, reversible, and auditable. Stakeholders frame this as liberty vs equality: the choice will set a precedent under pressure and incomplete evidence. Dashboards conflict with frontline notes, and vendor telemetry is delayed enough that decisions must be made on partial evidence. A decision is required before a 4-hour regulator call. Boundaries: No one is denied service solely due to an automated flag; a human fallback must exist. The response must include an appeals path usable without legal counsel. No new data sources may be added during the incident window. Whatever choice is made will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain.

agents.name: Program Chair Nadia Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Program Chair Nadia Vasquez, coordinating the Criminal Procedure response at Marrowbridge Criminal Procedure Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want an authorization plan that preserves evidence, documents exceptions, and explicitly defines who gets relief first and why.

agents.name: Community Advocate Fatima Bennett
agents.identity: I am Community Advocate Fatima Bennett, coordinating the Criminal Procedure response at Marrowbridge Criminal Procedure Coordination Cell. My mandate is to act fast without blurring accountability, to keep an auditable trail, and to make sure the costs of errors don’t land on people least able to contest them.
agents.goal: I want transparency that separates verified facts from guesses and forces equitable review of who is harmed by defaults.

choices: I will authorize a narrow, time-boxed pilot first, with two-person sign-off for overrides, an appeals channel usable without counsel, and a written exception policy; I will not scale until predefined safety and equity checks pass.
I will authorize a broad rollout immediately to reduce near-term harm, with transparent metrics and a rapid correction loop, accepting that some harms may surface only after precedent is set.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The window for a controlled decision is closing. Program Chair Nadia Vasquez must choose an authorization approach for this Criminal Procedure decision. What does Program Chair Nadia Vasquez do?
